creator: darwinsurvivor@gmail.com
text:
The link below does not work (No PRs Matched Query). I have been e-mailing 
with Ross Timson who has been trying to get the port updated to the 
current release. He said he simply bumped the version number for the 
package, so I believe the patch is most likely from him.

I am only the maintainer because there was no port for this application 
when I wished to install it and Ross has showed interest in maintaining 
the port, something I have no problems with since I simply currently do 
not have the time to maintain it myself.

I do not know the procedure for "passing on" a port to a new maintainer, 
if you could tell me the procedure or perform the action yourself, that 
would be great.


~Doug Penner
A.K.A. DarwinSurvivor



On Thu, 6 Dec 2012, Edwin Groothuis wrote:

> Maintainer of mail/notmuch,
>
> Please note that PR ports/174229 has just been submitted.
>
> If it contains a patch for an upgrade, an enhancement or a bug fix
> you agree on, reply to this email stating that you approve the patch
> and a committer will take care of it.
>
> The full text of the PR can be found at:
>    http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=ports/174229
>
> -- 
> Edwin Groothuis via the GNATS Auto Assign Tool
> edwin@FreeBSD.org
>
